amendments.reason: Phase 4 E2E surfaced P2-SAB-drift bug (harness commit pending): taskq.config and taskq.models were planned in P2 SAB but never implemented in P3. The harness now symmetrically enforces phantom-module detection at Gate 1 (_check_sab_module_alignment). Phantom modules removed; NFR-05/06 owners re-pointed to modules that actually own the work in the codebase.

NFR-03.target: atomic_write 100% across 3 JSON files; breaker OPEN→CLOSED <= TASKQ_BREAKER_COOLDOWN + 1s

NFR-05.target: docstring [FR-XX] coverage = 100% of public funcs/classes in src/taskq/
NFR-05.note: Was taskq.models under P2 SAB phantom planning; re-pointed to taskq.cli because docstring coverage is enforced as a codebase-wide property (every module contributes public defs with [FR-XX] tags), not as a single-module responsibility.

NFR-06.target: 8 TASKQ_* env vars exposed via typed getters in config.py
NFR-06.note: Was taskq.config under P2 SAB phantom planning; re-pointed to taskq.cli because the centralized config.py was never built. env vars are read in-place by the modules that consume them (store.TASKQ_HOME, breaker.TASKQ_BREAKER_*, executor timeout, etc.). config.py refactor is a P3+ extension item, not a P2/3 deliverable.
